records.display_name: Flatbush Ave Vision Zero priority corridor

payload.claim_text: Flatbush Ave is a Vision Zero priority corridor for safety improvements
payload.description: Flatbush Avenue is a Vision Zero priority corridor with 10 Vision Zero priority intersections. 164 people have been killed or seriously injured on Flatbush Ave in the last 5 years.

records.display_name: Subway and LIRR connections on Flatbush Ave

payload.claim_text: Connections to B/D, N/Q/R, 2/3, 4/5, S subways; Long Island Railroad
payload.description: Buses on Flatbush Ave provide critical connections to numerous subway lines and the Long Island Railroad

records.display_name: Recent and planned projects on Flatbush Avenue

payload.claim_text: NYCDOT has implemented or is planning numerous safety and operational projects on Flatbush recently, including, but not limited to: Congested Corridor Project (2015), Flatbush Avenue and Atlantic Avenue Pedestrian Improvements (2018), Hillel Place Plaza (2018 temporary, 2026 capital), Protected Bike Lane along Prospect Park (2020), Flatbush Ave/Utica Ave/Ave S Pedestrian Improvements (2024), Flatbush Avenue Safety Improvements, Livingston St to Pacific St Capital Project (2026)

records.display_name: Flatbush Avenue, Tillary Street to Avenue V, Brooklyn

description: Future bus priority corridor from NYC/MTA Transit Improvement Summit, NYC Streets Plan priority, Vision Zero priority corridor

Top-ranked NYC DOT Brooklyn bus priority corridor identified for potential bus priority street treatments. NYC DOT pursuing bus priority and pedestrian safety improvements between Tillary Street and Avenue V with over 118,000 daily bus riders on 12 routes pre-pandemic.
Flatbush Avenue corridor from Livingston Street to Grand Army Plaza, proposed for center-running bus lanes and bus priority improvements.
Northern section of Flatbush Avenue in Brooklyn where bus priority treatments are being implemented
Vision Zero Priority Corridor with center-running bus lanes proposed from Livingston St. to Grand Army Plaza

Critical transportation corridor in Brooklyn with connections to major job centers, housing, entertainment, culture, shopping, and recreation. Vision Zero Priority Corridor. Connects to Atlantic Terminal (LIRR, 9 subway lines), 2/5 trains at Nostrand Junction, B/Q/S trains at Prospect Park.
Downtown Brooklyn/Northern Flatbush section of Flatbush Avenue, focus of current bus priority design options

Northern section of Flatbush Avenue corridor focused for current bus priority design options
Vision Zero Priority Corridor with 12 bus routes, 132,000 daily riders, bus speeds as slow as 4 mph
Critical cross-borough transportation corridor connecting downtown Brooklyn to commercial hubs, cultural and educational institutions and medical care

Major corridor in Brooklyn with connections to major job centers, housing, entertainment, culture, shopping, and recreation. Atlantic Terminal provides connections to LIRR trains and 9 subway lines. Intersects with 2/5 trains at Nostrand Junction, B/Q/S trains at Prospect Park.

Critical cross-borough transportation corridor connecting downtown Brooklyn to commercial hubs, cultural and educational institutions and medical care. Three major sections: Tillary St to Empire Blvd, Empire Blvd to Nostrand Ave, Nostrand Ave to Kings Plaza.
SBS candidate corridor identified for next phase of SBS program
Major corridor in Brooklyn with connections to major job centers, housing, entertainment, culture, shopping, and recreation. Vision Zero Priority Corridor.

Northern section of Flatbush Avenue corridor, focus of current bus priority design options

records.display_name: Flatbush Avenue Bus Priority, Brooklyn

description: Investigating bus priority and pedestrian safety improvements on Flatbush Avenue between Tillary Street and Avenue V. Flatbush Avenue is a future bus priority corridor from the NYC/MTA Transit Improvement Summit, a priority corridor in the NYC Streets Plan, and a Vision Zero priority corridor.

Community Advisory Board presentation of draft proposal for center-running bus lanes, bus boarding islands, pedestrian improvements, and loading zones on Flatbush Avenue from Livingston St. to Grand Army Plaza
Proposal for center-running bus lanes, bus boarding islands, curb management, and pedestrian safety improvements on Flatbush Avenue from Livingston Street to Grand Army Plaza, Brooklyn.
Center-running physically protected bus lanes on Flatbush Avenue between State Street and Grand Army Plaza, with painted pedestrian spaces, parking/loading updates, and Pacific Street reversal
Proposed center-running bus lanes, bus boarding islands, curb management, bus stop consolidation, and route changes on Flatbush Avenue between Dean Street and Grand Army Plaza
Proposal for center-running bus lanes on Flatbush Avenue from Livingston St to Grand Army Plaza, with future phases to study other sections
Proposed center-running bus lanes, bus boarding islands, curb management, pedestrian space, and bike parking on Flatbush Avenue between Atlantic Avenue and Grand Army Plaza, Brooklyn
Center-running bus lanes on Flatbush Avenue from Livingston St to Grand Army Plaza with bus boarding islands, pedestrian safety improvements, and curb management

Bus priority project on Flatbush Avenue in Brooklyn, focusing on Downtown Brooklyn/Northern Flatbush section in current phase. Three design options being studied: curbside bus lanes, offset bus lanes, and center-running bus lanes.

Current round of design options focuses on Downtown Brooklyn/Northern Flatbush corridor section. Three design options being studied: curbside bus lanes, offset bus lanes, and center-running bus lanes. DOT plans to study bus priority treatments in other sections in future phases.
Bus priority project on Flatbush Avenue in Brooklyn, led by NYCDOT and MTA, with community advisory board process

Bus priority project on Flatbush Avenue in Brooklyn, launched with a kickoff meeting on June 30, 2022. Part of the next round of bus priority projects announced at the Transit Improvement Summit on June 16, 2022.
Bus priority improvements on Flatbush Avenue including curbside bus lanes, offset bus lanes, and center-running bus lanes. Current round focuses on Downtown Brooklyn/Northern Flatbush section.

Bus priority improvements on Flatbush Avenue focusing on Downtown Brooklyn/Northern Flatbush section. Three design options being studied: curbside bus lanes, offset bus lanes, and center-running bus lanes.

Bus priority project on Flatbush Avenue, a critical cross-borough transportation corridor in Brooklyn. Part of the Transit Improvement Summit announced June 16, 2022 by Mayor Eric Adams and MTA Chair Janno Lieber.
Center-running bus lanes on Flatbush Avenue from Livingston St to Grand Army Plaza, with future phases to study other sections

Bus priority project on Flatbush Avenue from Tillary Street to Kings Plaza through downtown Brooklyn to southern Brooklyn. A collaborative effort between NYC DOT and MTA to improve transit service, including bus lanes, busways, automated enforcement, and transit signal priority.
